original: First airport for electric flying cars and large drones, built by Urban-Air Port, begins operations in Coventry, England
Francis CrickandJames Watson's discovery of the double helix structure of DNA is published in "Nature" magazine
Hubble space telescope is placed into orbit by space shuttle Discovery
Buddy, a German Shepherd, becomes 1st guide dog for a US citizen Morris Frank
NASA space probe Pioneer 10 travels beyond Pluto's orbit
Japanese M1 spacecraft crashes on the moon, in its attempt to become the first privately-owned spacecraft to land on the Moon
Bell labs announces the first solar battery made from silicon. It has about 6% efficiency.
In last of 9th, Detroit Tigers, trailing by 13-4, score 10 runs to win one of greatest comebacks in baseball (1st game in Detroit)
(Meadow) "Meadowlark" Lemon, American basketball star (Harlem Globetrotters, 1955-80, 1990, and 3 other teams), and clergyman, born in Wilmington, North Carolina (d. 2015)
Hans-Werner Grosse glides 907.7 miles (1,461 km) in an AS-W-12
Wayne Gretzkyscores 7 points in a Cup game for the second time (3 goals, 4 assists)
Penguin Mario Lemuix ties NHL playoff record of 4 goals in 1st period
Cub centerfielder Rick Monday rescues US flag from 2 fans trying to set it on fire
Chuck Cooper becomes the 1st African American to be drafted into the NBA (for Boston Celtics)
Yuri Andropovinvites US schoolgirl Samantha Smith to the Soviet Union
US Ranger spacecraft crash lands on Moon
Rare daylight meteor observed from New Jersey, US to Quebec, Canada in the early evening
Robert Noyce patents integrated circuit
Daniel Defoepublishes "Robinson Crusoe", regarded as the 1st English novel
Paul Julius Reuter sets up carrier-pigeon service, using 40 pigeons to carry stock market prices between Aachen and Brussels
First submerged circumnavigation of Earth is completed by the USS submarine Triton in 60 days and 21 hours
14 inches of snow falls in Southern California
German cartographer Martin Waldseemüller is the first to use the name America on his world map "Universalis Cosmographia"
St Lawrence Seaway linking Atlantic and the Great Lakes opens to shipping
NASA launches space vehicle S-211
NFL moves goal posts & adopts sudden-death playoff
Cleveland sends Harry Chiti to the Mets for a player to be named later; on June 15, the Mets send Chiti back to Cleveland
Mercury-Atlas 3 rocket lifts off with a robot electronic mannequin inside the capsule and goes straight up forcing NASA to destroy it 43 seconds into the mission
Clandestine Radio 1212, used to hoax Nazi Germany's final transmission
Final piece of the Obelisk of Axum is returned to Ethiopia after being stolen by the invading Italian army in 1937
Cyclone Kenneth strikes Mozambique with winds of 220km/h (140mph) killing at least38 people and damaging nearly 35,000 homes
Record April temperatures continue across Thailand, Myanmar, Laos and Vietnam, as Myanmar city of Theinzayet registers record 43C (109.4F)

John Bevins Moisant, American pioneer aviator, first to cross English Channel with passenger and a cat, killed in New Orleans whose present day airport was originally name for him, born in Kankakee, Illinois (d. 1910)
Jean Purdy, British embryologist and nurse (pioneer in developing IVF), born in Cambridge, England (d. 1985)
Wolfgang Pauli, Swiss-American theoretical physicist (Pauli inhibition, Nobel 1945), born in Vienna, Austria-Hungary (d. 1958)
James Peebles, Canadian American astrophysicist (Ostriker–Peebles criterion), 2019 Nobel Prize for Physics, born in Winnipeg, Manitoba
Ron Clements, American animation director (Moana), born in Sioux City, Iowa
Vladislav Tretiak, Russian hockey player (Olympic gold 1972, 76), born in Dmitrovsky District, Russia
NFL Draft: Oklahoma quarterback Kyler Murray #1 pick by Arizona Cardinals
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: 3-peat for the Toronto Maple Leafs with a 4-0 Game 7 win over Detroit Red Wings for a 4-3 series victory
Peace treaty between Israel & Egypt goes into effect
Violeta Chamorro begins a 6-year term as Nicaragua's 1st female president
Charlotte Brontë’s "A Book of Ryhmes" written when she was 13, sells at auction in New York for $1.25 million to Friends of the National Libraries for theBrontëParsonage Museum
Charles Sumner Tainter, American inventor of sound-recording instruments (graphophone, dictaphone), born in Watertown, Massachusetts (d. 1940)[1]
John Henry "Pop" Lloyd, American Baseball HOF shortstop (career batting average: .349 NgLs) and manager (Lincoln Giants, Bacharach Giants), born in Palatka, Florida (d. 1964)
Randy Cross, American NFL player (San Francisco 49ers; 3x Super Bowl champion, 3x First-team All-pro, 3x Pro Bowl) and football analyst, born in Brooklyn, New York
Angel Martino, American 50m/100m freestyle swimmer (Olympic gold/2 br-92, 96), born in Tuscaloosa, Alabama
Last Boeing B-17 attack against Nazi Germany
Lion & Tiger Veldt at Cleveland Metroparks Zoo is dedicated (Cleveland, Ohio)
Battle at Dan-no-ura: Major sea battle in Japan's Genpei War, Minamoto clan led by Minamoto no Yoshitsune defeats the Taira clan
Fraser River Gold Rush begins in British Columbia as miners arrive from California
Ground broken for Suez Canal
"La Marseillaise", later the national anthem of France, is composed by Claude Joseph Rouget de Lisle in Strasbourg
1st experimental sodium nuclear reactor operated
Erve Beck hits American League's 1st home run
Philadelphia Phillies' Dick Bartell is 1st MLB player to get 4 consecutive doubles in 9 innings
Lou Theszbeats Whipper Watson in St Louis, to win National Wrestling Association world heavyweight title
"Bring in Da Noise, Bring in Da Funk" opens at Ambassador Theater NYC
Holland-Dozier-Holland's Invictus Records releases Freda Payne's signature single "Band of Gold"
US canal rights in Nicaragua and rights to Corn Islands expire
